symptoms: Rectangular gray-tan lesions parallel to leaf veins
Tan/gray center with dark brown borders
Entire leaf death in severe cases
treatment: Apply Propiconazole or Azoxystrobin fungicide at first sign
Remove and destroy infected debris
Improve air circulation
prevention: Crop rotation — no corn same field 2 years

symptoms: Small circular to elongated brown pustules on both leaf surfaces
Pustules turn black as season progresses
Leaves yellow and die in severe infection
treatment: Apply Mancozeb or Trifloxystrobin fungicide
Scout fields regularly — treat early
prevention: Early planting avoids peak rust season

symptoms: Long cigar-shaped gray-green lesions 1-6 inches
Tan to gray color with wavy edges
Dark green water-soaked borders initially
treatment: Apply Propiconazole (Tilt) or Pyraclostrobin at VT/R1 stage
Remove infected leaves
prevention: Use resistant hybrids with Ht genes

expert_resources.title: Northern Corn Leaf Blight — Iowa State

symptoms: No disease symptoms
treatment: No treatment needed

symptoms: Circular tan-brown spots with dark borders on leaves
Black mummified berries remaining on vine
Reddish-brown lesions on shoots
treatment: Apply Myclobutanil or Mancozeb — begin at budbreak
Remove all mummified berries and infected leaves
Prune vines for better air circulation
prevention: Apply fungicides on 7-10 day schedule during wet season

expert_resources.title: Black Rot of Grape — Penn State

symptoms: Tiger-stripe pattern on leaves — yellow/red between veins
Dark spots on berries (measles appearance)
Sudden vine collapse in summer
Dark streaking in wood when cut
causes: Fungal complex: Phaeomoniella chlamydospora + Phaeoacremonium spp.
treatment: No cure — manage to slow progression
Remove and burn severely infected vines
Apply wound protectant paste after pruning
prevention: Apply Trichoderma-based biological protectant to pruning wounds
Delay pruning to later in dormant season

expert_resources.title: Esca and Black Measles — UC Davis

symptoms: Angular dark brown spots on upper leaf surface
Grayish growth on undersides
Premature defoliation in severe cases
causes: Warm humid conditions late in season
treatment: Apply copper-based fungicide or Mancozeb
Remove fallen leaves from around vines

symptoms: No disease symptoms
treatment: No treatment needed

symptoms: Yellow blotchy mottle on leaves — asymmetric pattern
Small lopsided bitter fruit
Premature fruit drop
Twig dieback
Corky veins on leaves
causes: Spread by Asian citrus psyllid insect
treatment: Remove and destroy infected trees immediately
Control psyllid with systemic Imidacloprid
Apply nutritional supplements to extend life of mildly infected trees
prevention: Monitor and control Asian citrus psyllid
Quarantine new plants before introducing to grove

symptoms: Small water-soaked spots that turn purple-brown
Spots fall out leaving shot-hole appearance
Sunken dark spots on fruit surface
Cankers on twigs
causes: Spread by rain and wind during warm wet weather
treatment: Apply copper-based bactericide early in season
Apply Oxytetracycline during bloom period
Prune infected twigs during dry weather
prevention: Apply dormant copper spray before budbreak

symptoms: No disease symptoms
treatment: No treatment needed

symptoms: Small water-soaked spots on leaves and fruit
Spots turn brown with yellow halos
Raised scab-like lesions on fruit
Defoliation in severe cases
causes: Spread through infected seed and transplants
treatment: Apply copper fungicide + Mancozeb combination spray
Remove heavily infected plants
prevention: Avoid working in field when wet

symptoms: No disease symptoms
treatment: No treatment needed

symptoms: Dark brown concentric ring spots (target-board pattern) on older leaves
Yellow halo around spots
Sunken dark spots on tubers
causes: Warm 75-85F with alternating wet and dry periods
Affects stressed or nutrient-deficient plants more severely
treatment: Apply Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb at 7-10 day intervals
Remove infected leaves
Improve plant nutrition — nitrogen deficiency worsens disease
prevention: Crop rotation with non-solanaceous crops for 3 years

symptoms: Water-soaked pale green to brown spots on leaves
White fluffy sporulation on underside in humid conditions
Rapid browning and death of foliage
Brown rot on tubers with reddish-brown internal discoloration
causes: Spreads very rapidly — can destroy entire field in days
treatment: Apply Metalaxyl + Mancozeb or Cymoxanil IMMEDIATELY
Remove and destroy infected plants — do not compost
Do not harvest tubers during wet conditions
prevention: Apply preventive fungicide before disease appears in wet seasons
Hill up soil around plants to protect tubers

expert_resources.title: Late Blight of Potato — Cornell University

symptoms: No disease symptoms
treatment: No treatment needed

symptoms: No disease symptoms
treatment: No treatment needed

symptoms: No disease symptoms
treatment: No treatment needed

symptoms: White powdery spots on leaves and stems
Older leaves affected first
Yellowing and drying of leaves
Reduced fruit quality and yield
causes: Fungus: Podosphaera xanthii and Erysiphe cichoracearum
Dry weather with high humidity at night
Does not require wet leaves to spread
treatment: Apply potassium bicarbonate or sulfur-based fungicide
Neem oil spray as organic option
Remove heavily infected leaves
prevention: Water at the base — avoid wetting foliage

symptoms: Small purple spots on upper leaf surface
Spots enlarge and centers turn brown
Entire leaf turns brown and dry (scorch) in severe cases
Leaf edges curl upward
treatment: Apply Captan or Myclobutanil fungicide
Remove old infected leaves after harvest
prevention: Use drip irrigation instead of overhead

symptoms: No disease symptoms
treatment: No treatment needed

symptoms: Small water-soaked spots turning dark brown with yellow halos
Raised scab-like lesions on fruit
Leaf yellowing and defoliation
causes: Warm 75-86F with rain and overhead irrigation
treatment: Apply copper bactericide + Mancozeb spray
Remove infected plant material
prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds and transplants

symptoms: Concentric ring spots (bulls-eye pattern) on older leaves
Yellow halo around brown lesions
Dark cankers at soil level on stems
causes: Warm moist weather with heavy dew
treatment: Apply Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb every 7 days
Remove affected lower leaves
prevention: Mulch around plants to prevent soil splash
Stake plants to improve air circulation

symptoms: Large irregular water-soaked gray-green patches on leaves
White mold on underside in humid weather
Brown greasy spots on fruit
Rapid plant collapse
causes: Entire field can be destroyed in 7-10 days
treatment: Apply Metalaxyl + Mancozeb IMMEDIATELY upon detection
Remove and burn all infected plants
Do not compost infected material
prevention: Apply preventive fungicide in high-risk weather
Destroy crop debris thoroughly after season

symptoms: Pale green or yellow spots on upper leaf surface
Olive-green to brown velvety mold on underside
Leaves curl upward and die in severe cases
causes: High humidity above 85% in greenhouses or dense canopies
treatment: Apply Chlorothalonil or copper-based spray
Improve ventilation and reduce humidity
prevention: Avoid excess nitrogen which promotes dense foliage

symptoms: Small circular spots with white/gray centers and dark borders
Tiny black pycnidia dots in center of spots
Starts on lower leaves and moves upward
Severe defoliation
causes: Overwinters in infected plant debris and weeds
treatment: Apply Chlorothalonil or copper-based fungicide
Remove lower infected leaves

symptoms: Fine stippling (tiny yellow dots) on upper leaf surface
Bronze or yellow discoloration
Fine webbing on leaf undersides
Tiny moving dots visible under magnification
causes: Hot dry conditions favor rapid reproduction
Dust and drought stress increase susceptibility
treatment: Apply Abamectin or Bifenazate miticide
Neem oil or insecticidal soap as organic option
Increase humidity — mites dislike moist conditions
Release predatory mites (Phytoseiulus persimilis) for biocontrol
prevention: Maintain adequate irrigation during hot dry weather
Avoid broad-spectrum insecticides that kill natural predators

symptoms: Small brown spots with concentric rings forming target pattern
Spots enlarge and merge causing large dead areas
Dark brown spots with yellow halo on fruit
treatment: Apply Azoxystrobin or Chlorothalonil fungicide
Improve air circulation by pruning
prevention: Stake plants to keep off ground

symptoms: Upward curling and yellowing of young leaves
Stunted plant growth
Reduced fruit set and yield
Thickened leathery leaf texture
causes: Virus: Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus (TYLCV)
No cure — plant must be removed
treatment: Remove and destroy infected plants immediately
Control whitefly with Imidacloprid or Thiamethoxam
Use yellow sticky traps to monitor whitefly levels
prevention: Cover young plants with insect-proof mesh

symptoms: Light and dark green mottled pattern on leaves
Leaf distortion and curling
Stunted growth
Reduced fruit quality
causes: Spreads through contact — hands tools infected plants
treatment: No cure — remove infected plants
Disinfect tools with 10% bleach solution between plants
Wash hands thoroughly before handling plants

symptoms: No disease symptoms
treatment: No treatment needed
